title: Money personality
description: Practice understanding of money personalities and strategies associated with each.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are four general money personalities: spender, saver, balancer, and investor.

**What is the main characteristic of an *investor*? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They're always looking for ways to cut costs.
They love using their money.
They're interested in making their money grow.
They're careful to divide their money among saving, spending, and investing.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are four general money personalities: spender, saver, balancer, and investor.

**What is the main characteristic of a *spender*? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They like to shop for things they want and need, even if they don't have enough money.
They like to put aside money for future goals and emergencies, and avoid unnecessary expenses.
They like to grow money in different ways to increase their wealth and achieve financial independence.
They like to keep track of their income and expenses and make a budget to meet both their needs and wants.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are four general money personalities: spender, saver, balancer, and investor.

**What is the main characteristic of a *saver*? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They plan ahead for their money goals and set aside some of their income every month.
They enjoy buying new things and having fun with their money, even if it means going into debt.
They use their money to try different things so they can make more money.
They watch where their money goes very closely, and are equally good at spending, saving, and investing.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are four general money personalities: spender, saver, balancer, and investor.

**What is the main characteristic of a *balancer*? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They like to plan how to use their money wisely, and set aside some for savings and some for fun.
They like to buy new things often, even if they have to borrow money or use credit cards.
Their main goal is making their money grow fast, even if it comes with great risk.
They frequently put money aside and only spend on needs, rarely on wants.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each money personality—whether it be saver, spender, investor, or balancer—comes with its own set of pros and cons. 

**For someone who tends to be a *saver*, what is one potential drawback they may need to watch out for?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They might miss out on opportunities to make their money grow.
They often spend impulsively.
They're at risk of losing money.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each money personality—whether it be saver, spender, investor, or balancer—comes with its own set of pros and cons. 

**For someone who tends to be an *investor*, what is one potential drawback they may need to be aware of?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They may take on too much risk and lose money in the market.
They often end up in debt.
They may be too focused on their financial goals and setting money aside that they do not get to enjoy their present life.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each money personality—whether it be saver, spender, investor, or balancer—comes with its own set of pros and cons. 

**For someone who tends to be a *spender*, what is one potential drawback they may need to be aware of?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They may not have enough money for emergencies or long-term goals.
They tend to get stressed or guilty when trying to find best ways to manage their money.
They may miss out on opportunities to grow their money even more through investments.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each money personality—whether it be saver, spender, investor, or balancer—comes with its own set of pros and cons. 

**For someone who tends to be a *balancer*, what is one potential drawback they may need to watch out for?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They may struggle to find the right mix of saving, spending, and investing.
They may be too cautious and miss out on potential returns.
They may be too optimistic and underestimate the risks involved in trying to grow money too quickly.

assessment_items.question_content:
Carlos loves spending his money. He doesn't really think twice about buying something he wants, even if he has to go into debt to do it. 

**What money personality is Carlos most likely to have?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: spender
saver
investor
balancer

assessment_items.question_content:
Leah always puts her tax return and birthday money into a separate account. She likes to see her money grow steadily over time, and she only spends it on things that are necessary.

**What money personality is Leah most likely to have?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: spender
saver
investor
balancer

assessment_items.question_content:
CeCe likes to invest, save, and spend their money. They have a budget that they follow, and they set aside some money for goals, some for investments, and some for fun. They are not afraid to spend money on things they enjoy, but they also do not overspend or borrow money. 

**What money personality is CeCe most likely to have?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: spender
saver
investor
balancer

assessment_items.question_content:
Maya loves to learn about different ways to make money. She uses some of her money to buy stocks, bonds, and other assets that can increase in value over time. She is also thinking about starting her own business to sell products and services.

 **What money personality is Maya most likely to have?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: spender
saver
investor
balancer

assessment_items.question_content:
Josh is a saver type. He always looks for the best deals and is hesitant to take risks. 

**What is a good strategy for Josh to make the most of his money?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Invest in high-risk stocks so he can earn a higher return on his money.
Create a budget that is a little less strict so he can enjoy spending some of his money on things that make him happy.
Put all his money into a bank account so he doesn't have to worry about losing it.
Go on a shopping spree and buy whatever he wants.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kara is a spender type. She loves to buy new things and enjoy life, but sometimes she overspends and struggles to save. 

**What is a good strategy for Kara to manage her money better?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: She should set a realistic budget and track her spending habits.
She should invest all her money in the stock market and enjoy big returns.
She should buy whatever she wants since she is a spender.
Avoid shopping altogether and save all her money.

assessment_items.question_content:
Leo is an investor type. He likes to research and analyze different options and opportunities. He is willing to take risks for higher returns. 

**What is a good habit for Leo to practice regularly?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He should invest in multiple and different types of investments, instead of a single type.
He should follow his intuition and trust his gut feelings.
He should withdraw all his money as soon as he sees a profit.
He should ignore the advice of experts and professionals.

assessment_items.question_content:
Omar is a balancer type. He is good at managing his money and has a healthy balance between spending and saving. He also likes to donate to causes he cares about and share his wealth with others. 

**What is a good strategy for Omar to continue his financial well-being?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He can look at his money goals and plans often and change them if he needs to.
He should stop saving and spend all his money on himself and others.
He should stop donating and save and invest all his money for the future.
He can forget about his money and let it do whatever it wants.
